componentDescription: NumberInput is a text input specialized for numeric values with optional increment/decrement stepper buttons. Use it for quantities, prices, ages, counts, and any field requiring a precise numeric value. For ranges with a visual slider, use Slider instead.

usageExamples.title: Quantity selector
usageExamples.description: Order quantity input with min 1 and max based on stock.
usageExamples.code:
<NumberInput
  label="Quantity"
  value={quantity}
  onChange={setQuantity}
  min={1}
  max={product.stockCount}
  step={1}
  size={TextInputSize.MEDIUM}
/>

usageExamples.title: Price input with decimal step
usageExamples.description: Price field accepting decimal values with 0.01 increment.
usageExamples.code:
<NumberInput
  label="Price (USD)"
  value={price}
  onChange={setPrice}
  min={0}
  step={0.01}
  size={TextInputSize.MEDIUM}
  error={price <= 0 && submitted}
  errorMessage="Price must be greater than 0"
/>

value.llmContext: Required. Current numeric value. Manage with useState. Initialize to a sensible default (not NaN or undefined). For optional fields, use null and handle the null case in onChange.

onChange.llmContext: Required. Callback receiving the new number when the user types or clicks increment/decrement. Validate the value against min/max in your handler if needed.
onChange.propDescription: Required. Called with new numeric value on change

min.llmContext: Minimum allowed value. The decrement button is disabled when value equals min. For quantities, use 1 (not 0 for required items) or 0 for optional counts. For prices, use 0 or a minimum price floor.

max.llmContext: Maximum allowed value. The increment button is disabled when value equals max. Use for stock limits, seat counts, or any field with a hard ceiling.

step.llmContext: Amount added/subtracted per increment/decrement click. Use 1 for whole number values, 0.01 for currency, 0.1 for percentages or measurements with 1 decimal place.

error.llmContext: Set to true for validation error state. Pair with errorMessage. Trigger after form submit or on blur. Common errors: value below min, above max, or non-integer when integer is required.
error.propDescription: Shows error styling on the input
